qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: KMIP XML parser resolves attacker-controlled external DTDs and discloses local files
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory fixes unbounded AEAD chunk-size handling in OpenPGP `bcpg` code (`AEADEncDataPacket` and related `PGPAEADUtil` callers). This finding is an XXE in `kmip/.../KMIPInputStream` caused by default XML external-entity resolution, so the affected module, parser state, and root cause do not overlap.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The session's only final qualifying finding is a reproduced KMIP XML external-entity issue. GHSA-cj8j-37rh-8475 is instead a pre-auth resource-exhaustion flaw caused by unbounded PGP AEAD chunk sizes in `bcpg`/OpenPGP packet handling, so no qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.
evidence: The advisory summary and wiki describe `Unbounded PGP AEAD chunk size leads to pre-auth resource exhaustion.`

The advisory fix commit `dc7530939ffb6cdb57636f3609d98e23b94e71c1` adds chunk-size range checks in `pg/src/main/java/org/bouncycastle/bcpg/AEADEncDataPacket.java` and related OpenPGP AEAD utility code.

The only qualifying finding report targets `kmip/src/main/java/org/bouncycastle/kmip/wire/KMIPInputStream.java` and describes XML external DTD/entity resolution leading to XXE-based local-file disclosure.
